title: ophiorrhiziphyllon diandrum
intro: Ophiorrhiziphyllon diandrum, also known as the 'Two-flowered Snake-bark Vine', is a plant species in the family Gentianaceae, native to China and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Ophiorrhiziphyllon diandrum include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are commonly used to make infusions and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
